description: Direct relayer repayment of the refunds stranded by the 2026-07-17 incident's two abandoned root bundles on the Solana SpokePool. Single SVM relayer refund leaf, amountToReturn 0; 236.090934 USDC stays in the vault backing the 5 outstanding ClaimAccounts. The full available amount goes to E4bX: CBG4's stranded 734.833656 was already compensated off-protocol on 2026-07-17 (see cbg4Compensation). See README.md for the first-principles derivation and stranded_fills.csv for the per-fill evidence.

hashDetail.note: SVM leaf hash = keccak256(64 zero bytes || borsh(amountToReturn u64, chainId u64, refundAmounts Vec<u64>, leafId u32, mintPublicKey [32], refundAddresses Vec<[32]>)). Single leaf => merkle root = leaf hash, proof = [].

strandedEntitlements.note: Per-fill breakdown in stranded_fills.csv. CBG4's 734.833656 was already compensated off-protocol (see cbg4Compensation), so the leaf pays the full available 13,832.309134 to E4bX; its remaining 50,320.882173 is a claim against the HubPool, settled off-chain.

liveStateSnapshot.claimAccountsNote: Each pubkey is the PDA ["claim_account", USDC mint, refundAddress] (refundAddress recovered from the claim's initialize_claim_account tx, initTx). ClaimAccount data stores no mint, so the verify script proves each account's mint by re-deriving the PDA from these refund addresses and fails on any claim account outside this set.

provenance.note: Bundle evaluation block ranges (incl. the Solana slot ranges above) are read from each ProposedRootBundle event's bundleEvaluationBlockNumbers in the proposal txs; roots relayed to the spoke are matched by relayerRefundRoot.

cbg4Compensation.summary: CBG4's stranded entitlement (734.833656) was compensated off-protocol on 2026-07-17: 735.50 USDC bridged Ethereum -> Solana via Mayan Swift, delivered 735.159139 USDC to CBG4's USDC ATA 37 minutes before the corrective leaf executed. Both legs verified on-chain below.

origin.note: Receipt (status success) shows 735.50 USDC moving 0x8372... -> Mayan forwarder 0x89c6340B... -> LiFi Diamond 0x1231DEB6... -> Mayan Swift 0x337685fd... in this tx's USDC Transfer logs.

destination.note: Mayan Swift RegisterOrder + Fulfill (program mayan34VedncxdK2XobtvWFDXQASUTBXhUVzt2kKgny); token balance delta +735.159139 USDC on the recipient token account.

senderIdentity.claim: The origin sender is an Across Council multisig signer (Risk Labs representative).
senderIdentity.repoReference: across-protocol/contracts script/safe-multisig/config.json lists the address as a Safe owner; the Across governance docs list it among the Council Safe's signers.

alternativeVariantTwoRecipients.note: Only if the CBG4 off-protocol compensation is ever unwound: refundAmounts ["13097475478", "734833656"], refundAddresses [E4bX, CBG4].

poisonedRootBundles.slowRelayRootNote: The slowRelayRoot is the bundle's single global tree, relayed identically to every spoke. 12662's non-zero root contains exactly one leaf: the slow-fill request for deposit 4160878 (Ethereum -> Base, 2.437661998616864616 WETH, request tx 0x3dc59c6d90391b8a7577e0cea01f82a1fffcd4b5e39cb3b5f47eb23112986bf9, 05:06:19 UTC — since fast-filled). Nothing under it is executable on Solana: execute_slow_relay_leaf rebuilds the leaf hash with the spoke's own state.chain_id (svm-spoke slow_fill.rs), so a Base leaf can never verify against the root here.

adminMessages.note: Three HubPool relaySpokePoolAdminFunction(34268394551451, message) calls, enqueued as one Safe batch. The two deletions close the poisoned root bundles relayed during the incident: their refund leaves (and 12662's slow-fill leaves) are otherwise permissionlessly executable against whatever the vault holds, including refunds to the attacker's forged-deposit wallets — a live replay risk. Confirm both deletions executed on Solana (verify.ts reports their PDA status) before executing the recovery leaf.

messages.purpose: emergencyDeleteRootBundle(12662) — poisoned bundle: refund root 0x34d80ac0..., slow relay root 0x09691006...

poisonedRootBundlesNote: SVM refund leaves aggregate one refund per relayer per (chain, token), so each poisoned bundle carries a single Solana USDC refund leaf with two entries (E4bX, CBG4). Contents reconstructed by aggregating all fills electing Solana repayment inside each bundle's evaluation window (~04:56-05:25 and ~05:25-05:56 UTC 2026-07-17; boundaries approximate to the minute, magnitudes exact to the fill set); E4bX's entries are dominated by repayments for fills of forged deposits, already written off in the incident books. amountToReturn values are the bundles' netSendAmounts. Execution requires vault balance >= the leaf's refund total (amountToReturn is NOT balance-checked; it queues TransferLiability), so neither leaf is executable against today's ~14k — but the roots never expire: if the spoke is ever un-paused and holds working balances again, execution would double-pay written-off repayments and queue ~36.69M of phantom TransferLiability, letting any caller continuously bridge every future vault dollar to the HubPool. No Solana-executable slow-fill leaves exist (zero destination-Solana slow-fill requests in the windows; 12663's slow root is zero). Deleting both bundles closes this permanently.
